item.title: GOOD
item.content:encoded: One of the best articles I have read, so tragic

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Plot Twist: Her name was never actually Elizabeth Connors, and she was a denizen of our universe all along. She's just a really good up-and-coming actress bought off by MC&D. They created the film reel, traded information about The Future for activities in The Past, and after they'd made all the money they feasibly could off of her, they groomed her for her greatest role of all— collecting the secrets of the SCP Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Liked it a lot, just a couple of things I think should be edited:

The initial time Elizabeth says what her date is, she says 1941… but I think it’s supposed to be 42

Also, I couldn’t shake the image of her pretending to be English upper class from the late 19th century during the first interview log: - her constantly saying “sir” at the end of many sentences and - her terminology like “Heavens” just smacks of a different period… this was the height of radio and golden age hollywood, but she seems to have an affectation during that first interview

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bro I want to see more of this.
Frankly I'd like to see SCP updates become more normalised but I've only seen it in 4020.
Still great job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last bit of dialogue gave me chills. This is masterful characterization, and an idea executed almost to perfection. +1 all the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God damn, that dialogue rules. I don't get invested in the characters in a lot of articles, but this one nailed it. Even though I predicted the sort of way it was going to end (though definitely not the specific way it went down), I was still gutted by the end.

Criminally underrated article at only +18.

Easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've seen this trope before, but something about the characterization and detail made it work for me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you, I appreciate the kind words.

Funny you should bring that up, because I actually considered a similar ending.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is awesome, but I'd love to see another Addendum where they try to run the tape again. Not that you have to include it, but I think it'd be really cool and tragic if Elizabeth appears on the tape again, but the timeline has been reset for her. You know what I mean?

Regardless, an excellent SCP, one of my favorites in recent memory.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really happy to see this one posted! Still gotta say it - I'm jealous of your dialogue skills.

A great read! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Proud of this one. Interested in seeing what people think of it.

Thanks to DrDromeus and Deadly Bread for test-reading it and critique.

Fingers crossed I didn't miss any grammatical errors.

Image Credit
